tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj tekst. Do każdej luki (1–6) dopasuj właściwe zdanie (A–H), aby powstał logiczny i spójny tekst. Dwa zdania zostały podane dodatkowo i nie pasują do żadnej luki.
tasks.text:
THE HISTORY OF CINEMA

The history of cinema is one of the most fascinating stories of human creativity and technological progress. It all began in the late nineteenth century, when inventors across Europe and America were racing to capture moving images on film. 1. _________ Their device, called the Cinematographe, was small enough to be carried and used both as a camera and a projector.

The first public screening took place in Paris in December 1895, where a small audience watched short clips of everyday life. 2. _________ Early audiences were so amazed by the moving pictures that some reportedly ran from the room in fright when they saw a train approaching on screen.

In the early years, films were silent and accompanied by live piano music in the theatre. Directors quickly learned how to tell stories through images alone, developing techniques such as close-ups and editing. 3. _________ This transformed cinema into a powerful storytelling medium that could reach millions of people.

The 1920s brought another revolution: sound. When The Jazz Singer was released in 1927, audiences heard an actor speak for the first time on screen. 4. _________ Hollywood studios invested heavily in new technology, and the golden age of cinema had truly begun.

Colour film became widely used in the 1950s, just as television was beginning to compete for audiences. 5. _________ Studios responded by making bigger, more spectacular productions that could not be replicated on a small screen at home.

Today, digital technology has completely changed how films are made and watched. 6. _________ Yet despite all these changes, the magic of sitting in a dark room and being transported to another world remains as powerful as ever.

options.text: Many actors who had become famous in silent films found that their voices did not suit the new talking pictures.
The French brothers Auguste and Louis Lumiere are widely credited with inventing practical cinema.
Those first paying customers had no idea they were witnessing the birth of a new art form.
Cinema attendance dropped sharply as more and more families bought television sets.
Special effects that once required enormous sets and hundreds of extras can now be created entirely by computer.
A breakthrough came when filmmakers discovered they could cut between different scenes to create suspense and drama.
Many countries around the world have developed their own distinctive national cinema traditions.
The cost of producing a major Hollywood film has risen dramatically over the past few decades.
